---
title: "project3"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

```{r}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyverse)
  library(survival)
  library(survminer)
  library(gtsummary)
  library(broom)
})
set.seed(12345)
```

```{r}
# 1. Load and filter baseline cohort
frm_raw  <- read.csv("frmgham2.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
baseline <- frm_raw %>% filter(PERIOD == 1, PREVSTRK == 0)
nrow(baseline)
```

```{r}
# 2. Construct 10-year event and follow-up time
baseline <- baseline %>%
  mutate(
    stroke_in_10y = as.integer(STROKE == 1 & TIMESTRK <= 3650),
    time10 = case_when(
      stroke_in_10y == 1           ~ TIMESTRK,
      DEATH == 1 & TIMEDTH <= 3650 ~ TIMEDTH,
      TRUE                         ~ pmin(TIMESTRK, TIMEDTH, 3650, na.rm = TRUE)
    ),
    event10 = stroke_in_10y,
    death10 = as.integer(DEATH == 1 & TIMEDTH <= 3650 & stroke_in_10y == 0),
    SEX_f      = factor(SEX,      levels = c(1, 2), labels = c("Men", "Women")),
    CURSMOKE_f = factor(CURSMOKE, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    DIABETES_f = factor(DIABETES, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    BPMEDS_f   = factor(BPMEDS,   levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    PREVCHD_f  = factor(PREVCHD,  levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    Stroke10_f = factor(event10,  levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    Death10_f  = factor(death10,  levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes"))
  )

c(strokes_10y = sum(baseline$event10),
  deaths_10y_no_stroke = sum(baseline$death10))
```

```{r}
# 3. Missing data
candidate_vars <- c("AGE", "SYSBP", "DIABETES", "CURSMOKE",
                    "PREVCHD", "BPMEDS", "TOTCHOL", "BMI")

baseline %>%
  select(all_of(candidate_vars)) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(is.na(.)))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "variable", values_to = "n_missing") %>%
  mutate(pct_missing = round(100 * n_missing / nrow(baseline), 2))
```

```{r}
# 4. Table 1: Baseline characteristics (full eligible cohort)
tab1_vars <- c("AGE", "SYSBP", "TOTCHOL", "BMI",
               "DIABETES_f", "CURSMOKE_f", "BPMEDS_f", "PREVCHD_f",
               "Stroke10_f", "Death10_f")

baseline %>%
  select(all_of(tab1_vars), SEX_f) %>%
  tbl_summary(
    by = SEX_f,
    statistic = list(all_continuous()  ~ "{mean} ({sd})",
                     all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)"),
    digits    = list(all_continuous()  ~ 1,
                     all_categorical() ~ c(0, 1)),
    missing      = "ifany",
    missing_text = "Missing",
    label     = list(
      AGE        ~ "Age (years)",
      SYSBP      ~ "Systolic BP (mmHg)",
      TOTCHOL    ~ "Total cholesterol (mg/dL)",
      BMI        ~ "BMI (kg/m^2)",
      DIABETES_f ~ "Diabetes mellitus",
      CURSMOKE_f ~ "Current smoker",
      BPMEDS_f   ~ "Antihypertensive medication",
      PREVCHD_f  ~ "Prevalent CHD",
      Stroke10_f ~ "Stroke within 10 years",
      Death10_f  ~ "Death within 10 years"
    )
  ) %>%
  add_overall() %>%
  modify_header(label = "**Characteristic**") %>%
  modify_caption("Table 1. Baseline characteristics of eligible participants by sex.") %>%
  modify_footnote(all_stat_cols() ~ "Mean (SD); n (%). Complete-case analysis excludes participants missing total cholesterol, antihypertensive medication, or BMI, yielding 1,897 men and 2,378 women.")
```

```{r}
# 5. SYSBP and diabetes across Periods 1-3
frm_raw %>%
  filter(RANDID %in% baseline$RANDID, PERIOD %in% 1:3) %>%
  group_by(PERIOD) %>%
  summarise(
    n           = n(),
    sysbp_mean  = round(mean(SYSBP, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    sysbp_sd    = round(sd(SYSBP,   na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    diabetes_n  = sum(DIABETES == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    diabetes_pc = round(mean(DIABETES == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 2),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

```{r}
# 6. Complete-case dataset for modeling
model_vars <- c("AGE", "SYSBP", "DIABETES", "CURSMOKE",
                "PREVCHD", "BPMEDS", "TOTCHOL", "BMI",
                "SEX", "time10", "event10")

cc    <- baseline %>% select(all_of(model_vars)) %>% drop_na()
men   <- cc %>% filter(SEX == 1)
women <- cc %>% filter(SEX == 2)

c(n_total = nrow(cc), n_men = nrow(men), n_women = nrow(women),
  events_men = sum(men$event10), events_women = sum(women$event10))
```

```{r fig.width=7, fig.height=5}
# 7. Kaplan-Meier curves by sex (Figure 1)
km_fit <- survfit(Surv(time10, event10) ~ SEX, data = cc)

ggsurvplot(
  km_fit, data = cc,
  conf.int      = TRUE,
  risk.table    = TRUE,
  legend.labs   = c("Men", "Women"),
  legend.title  = "",
  xlab          = "Days from baseline",
  ylab          = "Stroke-free survival probability",
  palette       = c("#2C7FB8", "#D95F0E"),
  xlim          = c(0, 3650),
  ylim          = c(0.90, 1.00),
  break.time.by = 730,
  ggtheme       = theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
)
```

```{r}
# 8a. Variable selection: per-sex backward AIC
# Forced: AGE, SYSBP, DIABETES (investigator specification)
run_selection <- function(dat) {
  full_fit <- coxph(
    Surv(time10, event10) ~ AGE + SYSBP + DIABETES + CURSMOKE +
                            PREVCHD + BPMEDS + TOTCHOL + BMI,
    data = dat
  )
  lower_fit <- Surv(time10, event10) ~ AGE + SYSBP + DIABETES
  step(full_fit,
       scope     = list(lower = lower_fit, upper = formula(full_fit)),
       direction = "backward", trace = 0)
}

selection_men   <- run_selection(men)
selection_women <- run_selection(women)

vars_men   <- attr(terms(selection_men),   "term.labels")
vars_women <- attr(terms(selection_women), "term.labels")

data.frame(
  candidate      = candidate_vars,
  retained_men   = candidate_vars %in% vars_men,
  retained_women = candidate_vars %in% vars_women
)
```

```{r}
# 8b. Confounding check: 10% change-in-estimate rule
check_confounding <- function(dat, final_vars, dropped_vars, threshold = 0.10) {
  base_fit <- coxph(reformulate(final_vars, response = "Surv(time10, event10)"), 
                    data = dat)
  base_hrs <- exp(coef(base_fit))
  
  result <- data.frame(dropped_var = character(), max_change = numeric(),
                       is_confounder = logical())
  
  for (v in dropped_vars) {
    test_fit <- coxph(reformulate(c(final_vars, v), 
                                  response = "Surv(time10, event10)"), 
                      data = dat)
    test_hrs <- exp(coef(test_fit))[names(base_hrs)]
    pct_change <- abs((test_hrs - base_hrs) / base_hrs)
    max_chg <- max(pct_change, na.rm = TRUE)
    
    result <- rbind(result, data.frame(
      dropped_var   = v,
      max_change    = round(max_chg, 3),
      is_confounder = max_chg > threshold
    ))
  }
  result
}

dropped_men   <- setdiff(candidate_vars, vars_men)
dropped_women <- setdiff(candidate_vars, vars_women)

conf_men   <- check_confounding(men,   vars_men,   dropped_men)
conf_women <- check_confounding(women, vars_women, dropped_women)

conf_men
conf_women
```

```{r}
# 9. FINAL models for inference (HR, PH tests come from these)
final_formula_men   <- reformulate(vars_men,   response = "Surv(time10, event10)")
final_formula_women <- reformulate(vars_women, response = "Surv(time10, event10)")

final_men   <- coxph(final_formula_men,   data = men)
final_women <- coxph(final_formula_women, data = women)

summary(final_men)
summary(final_women)
```

```{r}
# 10. Hazard ratio table (from AIC-selected final models)
fmt_hr <- function(fit, sex_label, n_obs, n_events) {
  tidy(fit, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
    transmute(
      Sex      = sex_label,
      n        = n_obs,
      events   = n_events,
      Variable = term,
      HR       = round(estimate,  3),
      LCL      = round(conf.low,  3),
      UCL      = round(conf.high, 3),
      p_value  = signif(p.value,  3)
    )
}

bind_rows(
  fmt_hr(final_men,   "Men",   nrow(men),   sum(men$event10)),
  fmt_hr(final_women, "Women", nrow(women), sum(women$event10))
)
```

```{r}
# 11. Proportional hazards assumption (on AIC-selected final models)
zph_men   <- cox.zph(final_men)
zph_women <- cox.zph(final_women)

zph_men
zph_women
```

```{r fig.width=9, fig.height=5}
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
plot(survfit(Surv(time10, event10) ~ DIABETES, data = men),
     fun = "cloglog", xlab = "log(days)", ylab = "log(-log S(t))",
     col = c("black", "red"), main = "Men: DIABETES")
legend("bottomright", c("No", "Yes"), col = c("black", "red"), lty = 1, bty = "n")
plot(survfit(Surv(time10, event10) ~ DIABETES, data = women),
     fun = "cloglog", xlab = "log(days)", ylab = "log(-log S(t))",
     col = c("black", "red"), main = "Women: DIABETES")
legend("bottomright", c("No", "Yes"), col = c("black", "red"), lty = 1, bty = "n")
```

```{r}
# 12. 10-year stroke probability: 5 symmetric scenarios for both sexes
# Extended model for women (scenario prediction only)
final_women_pred <- coxph(
  Surv(time10, event10) ~ AGE + SYSBP + DIABETES + CURSMOKE,
  data = women
)

ages <- c(40, 50, 60)

profile_grid <- tibble(
  scenario = c("No risk factors", "High BP (SBP=160)", "Diabetes",
               "High BP + diabetes", "Current smoking"),
  SYSBP    = c(120, 160, 120, 160, 120),
  DIABETES = c(  0,   0,   1,   1,   0),
  CURSMOKE = c(  0,   0,   0,   0,   1)
)

predict_10y <- function(fit, profiles_df, ages_vec) {
  grid <- profiles_df %>%
    slice(rep(seq_len(n()), each = length(ages_vec))) %>%
    mutate(AGE = rep(ages_vec, times = nrow(profiles_df)))
  sf     <- survfit(fit, newdata = grid)
  s_3650 <- summary(sf, times = 3650)$surv
  grid$prob_10y <- round((1 - as.numeric(s_3650)) * 100, 1)
  grid[, c("scenario", "AGE", "prob_10y")]
}

pred_men   <- predict_10y(final_men,        profile_grid, ages) %>% mutate(SEX = "Men")
pred_women <- predict_10y(final_women_pred, profile_grid, ages) %>% mutate(SEX = "Women")

bind_rows(pred_men, pred_women) %>%
  select(SEX, scenario, AGE, prob_10y) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = AGE, values_from = prob_10y, names_prefix = "Age_")
```

```{r fig.width=10, fig.height=5}
# 12b. Model-based survival curves at age 60 (using same models as Section 12)
curve_profiles <- profile_grid %>% mutate(AGE = 60)

sf_men_curve   <- survfit(final_men,        newdata = curve_profiles)
sf_women_curve <- survfit(final_women_pred, newdata = curve_profiles)

par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 3, 1))
cols <- c("#2C7FB8", "#41B6C4", "#7FCDBB", "#FEB24C", "#D95F0E")

plot(sf_men_curve, col = cols, lwd = 2,
     xlab = "Days from baseline", ylab = "Stroke-free survival probability",
     main = "Men (age 60)", ylim = c(0.70, 1.00), xlim = c(0, 3650))
legend("bottomleft", legend = curve_profiles$scenario,
       col = cols, lwd = 2, bty = "n", cex = 0.8)

plot(sf_women_curve, col = cols, lwd = 2,
     xlab = "Days from baseline", ylab = "Stroke-free survival probability",
     main = "Women (age 60)", ylim = c(0.70, 1.00), xlim = c(0, 3650))
legend("bottomleft", legend = curve_profiles$scenario,
       col = cols, lwd = 2, bty = "n", cex = 0.8)
```
